name: Chicken Stroganoff
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into strips
3 cups cooked brown rice or white rice
recipeInstructions:
This is a lighter version of the classic but it is very good.
Note: The original recipe called for 2 tablespoons oil. It didn't need that much.
1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Working in batches to avoid overcrowding, add chicken strips to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until golden brown and no longer pink in the center, about 6 minutes. Transfer chicken to a plate.
3. Reduce heat to medium. Add mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release juices and are golden and tender, about 6 minutes. If the skillet becomes too dry, add 1-2 teaspoons of chicken broth as needed.
4. In a measuring cup, stir together flour and 2 tablespoons milk until smooth with no lumps. Stir in remaining milk until well blended.
5. Return chicken to the skillet. Increase heat to medium-high. Add chicken broth.
6. Stir in flour mixture, salt, paprika, and black pepper into the skillet. Bring to a boil.
7. Reduce heat to medium; simmer, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens slightly, about 5 minutes.
8. Serve hot over cooked brown rice or white rice.
